task_completion.description: Verify that the sequence diagram contains the 7 required lifelines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the draw.io canvas. There should be exactly 7 vertical lifelines with the following headers: End User, Browser/SPA, Authorization Server, Token Endpoint, Resource Server, Session Store, and JWKS Endpoint.

task_completion.description: Verify that the diagram models the full interaction with appropriate messages, including PKCE and JWT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 15 message arrows connecting the lifelines. The labels on these arrows must include references to PKCE (challenge/verification), authorization code redirects, token exchange, and JWT validation.

task_completion.description: Verify the use of UML fragments for alternative outcomes and optional flows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for UML fragment boxes enclosing parts of the sequence diagram. There should be an 'alt' fragment (for alternative credential outcomes) and an 'opt' fragment (for optional refresh flows).

task_completion.description: Verify the existence of a second page titled 'Threat Model' with 5 security threats.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a second page/tab in the draw.io interface named 'Threat Model'. The page should contain a structured list of exactly 5 security threats.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project is saved as oauth2_sequence.drawio on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the draw.io window title bar for 'oauth2_sequence.drawio', or look at the Desktop directory in a file manager or terminal to see the saved file.

task_completion.description: Verify that the diagram is exported as oauth2_sequence.png on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Desktop directory in a file manager, terminal, or image viewer to confirm the existence of 'oauth2_sequence.png'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the draw.io GUI to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io interface open with the diagram being actively constructed or viewed, rather than just terminal commands generating the files.

integrity.description: Verify the PNG file matches the created diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the PNG is opened or previewed, it should visually match the diagram constructed in the draw.io editor, proving it was exported from the tool and not downloaded.

integrity.description: Verify the threat model was created in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The threat model should be visible as a page within the draw.io application, not just a separate text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The sequence diagram must contain exactly 7 lifelines: End User, Browser/SPA, Authorization Server, Token Endpoint, Resource Server, Session Store, and JWKS Endpoint. It should have at least 15 message edges and 20+ shapes. It must include UML fragments for 'alt' (alternative credential outcomes) and 'opt' (optional refresh flows). A second page titled 'Threat Model' must list exactly 5 threats: (1) Authorization Code Interception, (2) CSRF on Redirect, (3) Token Leakage, (4) Refresh Token Theft, (5) JWT Algorithm Confusion. The final outputs must be saved to ~/Desktop/oauth2_sequence.drawio and ~/Desktop/oauth2_sequence.png.
